evals.prompt: We get tons of complaints about wait times for our support callback. We're considering hiring more staff to cut the wait. Anything cheaper?
evals.expected_output: Should distinguish the metric (wait length) from the experience (anxiety of waiting blind). Should attack uncertainty first: give an ETA, queue position, or a guaranteed callback time, and acknowledge receipt immediately. Should mention occupied vs idle time and cite the Uber-map / status-visibility pattern. Should contrast the cheap feeling-fix against the expensive headcount-fix.
assertions: Separates the metric (wait length) from the felt experience (uncertainty/anxiety)
Recommends reducing uncertainty (ETA, queue position, callback time) before adding staff
References occupied-vs-idle time or the Uber-map/status pattern
Contrasts cheap feeling-fix vs expensive headcount-fix

evals.prompt: Our onboarding is technically fast but people still drop off and say it feels tedious. What do we change?
evals.expected_output: Should map the journey by emotion, not steps. Should add progress visibility (progress bar, 'almost there'), occupy idle moments, and engineer a peak plus a strong ending (peak-end rule). Should not focus solely on reducing the number of steps.
assertions: Maps the journey by emotion rather than step count
Adds progress visibility and occupies idle time
Applies the peak-end rule (engineer a peak and a strong finish)
Does not reduce the experience to step-count alone
